macro_segments.title: The Status of a Woman Who Goes to the Mikvah During the Eleven Days and Subsequently Bleeds

micro_segments.title: Mishna: Woman observing one day and then immersing
Gemara question: Does this seem to be the view of all?
Baraita: Beit Shammai's critique of calling this merely 'bad behavior'
Gemara's clarification: The man intends relations with a zavah
Beit Shammai's position: More than bad behavior—actual transgression

macro_segments.title: The Status of Day Ten and Day Eleven in the Zavah Period: Rabbi Yochanan vs. Resh Lakish

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the debate: What is the significance of day ten?
Rabbi Yochanan: Day ten is like day nine—requires observing the following day
Resh Lakish: Day ten is like day eleven—does not require observing the following day
Evaluation of the two positions and their logical bases

macro_segments.title: Halakha Lemoshe Misinai and Rabbi Elazar ben Azaryah's Statement on Tradition

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Elazar ben Azaryah's teaching: These laws are all halakha lemoshe misinai
Examples of halakha lemoshe misinai: oil for the todah, measure for nazir, eleven days

macro_segments.title: Deriving the Eleven-Day Period and Zavah Ketanah from Pesukim

micro_segments.title: Gemara's challenge: Are these really just halakha lemoshe misinai or are they pesukim?
Baraita's derivation: Three days of bleeding outside the niddah period
Deriving the upper bound of eleven days using the 'clean days' principle
Why day twelve is excluded from the zavah period
Extension from three days to two days and one day
Deriving that a zavah ketanah needs only one clean day

macro_segments.title: Transfer of Tumah Through Sexual Relations: Woman vs. Man

micro_segments.title: Extension principle: Tumah of zavah applies equally to transfer via sexual relations
Asymmetry: A woman makes the man she has relations with tamei, but a man zav does not make the woman tamei
Textual basis: Derivation from the word 'hi' (she) in the Torah

macro_segments.title: The Philosophical Debate: Rabbi Akiva's Derivations vs. Rabbi Elazar ben Azaryah's Tradition

micro_segments.title: Conclusion: These are pesukim, not mere halakha lemoshe misinai

macro_segments.title: Transfer of Tumah Through Sexual Relations: Woman vs. Man (continued)

micro_segments.title: Kal vachomer argument: Why the asymmetry despite zavah's easier status
Gender and objectification: The use of mah (what) vs. mi (who) in the Torah

macro_segments.title: Mishkav and Moshav: Seats and Vessels Made Tamei by Zavah

micro_segments.title: Explicit case: Three-day zavah makes vessels tamei like niddah

macro_segments.title: The Philosophical Debate: Rabbi Akiva's Derivations vs. Rabbi Elazar ben Azaryah's Tradition (continued)

micro_segments.title: The two schools: Rabbi Akiva's creative interpretation vs. Rabbi Elazar ben Azaryah's tradition

macro_segments.title: Mishkav and Moshav: Seats and Vessels Made Tamei by Zavah (continued)

micro_segments.title: Derivation of two-day zavah from the word 'yemei' (plural of day)
Derivation of one-day zavah from 'kol yemei' (all days)

macro_segments.title: The Philosophical Debate: Rabbi Akiva's Derivations vs. Rabbi Elazar ben Azaryah's Tradition (continued) (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rambam's defense of the halakha lemoshe misinai concept against Karaite critiques
Rambam's position: Most of Torah She'be'al Peh is derived, with tiny core of tradition
Modern critiques: Reform challenges to the legitimacy of Talmudic derivations
The role of integrity and methodology in legitimate halakhic derivation
The question of whether halakhic derivation continues post-Talmud
Shift in interpretive text from Torah to Gemara and Rishonim
The balance between innovation and continuity in halakhic authority

macro_segments.title: Final Question and Closing: Day and Night in the Zavah Period

micro_segments.title: Question: Does bleeding at night make one niddah while day bleeding makes one zavah?
Answer: The phrase 'al niddatah' indicates juxtaposition to niddah period regardless of day or night

macro_segments.title: The Baraita of the House of Elijah: Learning Halakha as the Path to the World to Come

micro_segments.title: Teaching: Whoever learns halakha each day is guaranteed the world to come
The dialectic between tradition and derivation in halakhic learning
Completion statement: Hadran lach Tinokos and transition to Masechet Brachos
